---
title: "XAI3"
author: "Maria, Alba"
date: "2026-04-28"
output: word_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
#install.packages("tidyverse")
#install.packages("randomForest")
#install.packages("pdp")
#install.packages("ggplot2")
```


```{r}
# Cargar librerías
library(tidyverse)
library(randomForest)
library(pdp)
library(ggplot2)

# Para que los resultados sean reproducibles
set.seed(123)

```


#LOAD AND PREPARE BIKE DATASET
```{r}
# Load the bike rental dataset
bike_data <- read.csv("day.csv")

# Display the first rows of the dataset
head(bike_data)

# Display column names
names(bike_data)

# Select relevant variables for the model
bike_model_data <- bike_data %>%
  select(cnt,instant,temp,hum, windspeed,season,yr,mnth,holiday,weekday, workingday,weathersit)

# Check the processed dataset
head(bike_model_data)

# Display statistical summary of the dataset
summary(bike_model_data)
```


#TRAIN RANDOM FOREST MODEL

```{r}
# Train the Random Forest model to predict bike rentals
bike_rf <- randomForest(
  cnt ~ .,              # Predict 'cnt' using all available features
  data = bike_model_data,
  ntree = 500,          # Number of trees in the forest
  importance = TRUE     # Compute variable importance
)

# Display model summary
print(bike_rf)

# Display variable importance values
importance(bike_rf)

# Plot variable importance
varImpPlot(bike_rf)
```

#PDP - DAYS SINCE 2011
```{r}
# Compute the Partial Dependence Plot for 'instant'
pdp_instant <- partial(
  object = bike_rf,
  pred.var = "instant",        # Variable to analyze
  train = bike_model_data,
  grid.resolution = 50         # Number of points to evaluate
)

# Plot the PDP
plot_instant <- autoplot(pdp_instant) +
  labs(
    title = "PDP - Influence of days since 2011",
    x = "Days since 2011",
    y = "Predicted bike rentals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Display the plot
print(plot_instant)
```



#PDP - TEMPERATURE

```{r}
# Compute the Partial Dependence Plot for 'temp'
pdp_temp <- partial(
  object = bike_rf,
  pred.var = "temp",          # Variable to analyze
  train = bike_model_data,
  grid.resolution = 50        # Number of evaluation points
)

# Plot the PDP
plot_temp <- autoplot(pdp_temp) +
  labs(
    title = "PDP - Influence of temperature",
    x = "Temperature",
    y = "Predicted bike rentals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Display the plot
print(plot_temp)
```

#PDP - HUMIDITY

```{r}
# Compute the Partial Dependence Plot for 'hum'
pdp_hum <- partial(
  object = bike_rf,
  pred.var = "hum",          # Variable to analyze
  train = bike_model_data,
  grid.resolution = 50       # Number of evaluation points
)

# Plot the PDP
plot_hum <- autoplot(pdp_hum) +
  labs(
    title = "PDP - Influence of humidity",
    x = "Humidity",
    y = "Predicted bike rentals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Display the plot
print(plot_hum)
```

#PDP - WIND SPEED

```{r}
# Compute the Partial Dependence Plot for 'windspeed'
pdp_wind <- partial(
  object = bike_rf,
  pred.var = "windspeed",    # Variable to analyze
  train = bike_model_data,
  grid.resolution = 50       # Number of evaluation points
)

# Plot the PDP
plot_wind <- autoplot(pdp_wind) +
  labs(
    title = "PDP - Influence of wind speed",
    x = "Wind speed",
    y = "Predicted bike rentals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Display the plot
print(plot_wind)
```


#PDP 2D - TEMPERATURE AND HUMIDITY

```{r}
# Take a random sample to reduce computational cost
bike_sample <- bike_model_data %>%
  sample_n(500)

# Compute 2D Partial Dependence Plot for temperature and humidity
pdp_temp_hum <- partial(
  object = bike_rf,
  pred.var = c("temp", "hum"),   # Variables to analyze jointly
  train = bike_sample,
  grid.resolution = 30           # Grid resolution for smoother surface
)

# Create heatmap (PDP 2D)
plot_temp_hum <- ggplot(pdp_temp_hum, aes(x = temp, y = hum, fill = yhat)) +
  geom_tile(width = 0.02, height = 0.02) +
  labs(
    title = "2D PDP - Temperature and Humidity",
    x = "Temperature",
    y = "Humidity",
    fill = "Prediction"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Display the plot
print(plot_temp_hum)

# Add real data distribution on top of the PDP
plot_temp_hum_density <- ggplot(pdp_temp_hum, aes(x = temp, y = hum, fill = yhat)) +
  geom_tile(width = 0.02, height = 0.02) +
  geom_point(
    data = bike_sample,
    aes(x = temp, y = hum),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    alpha = 0.3,
    size = 1
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "2D PDP with data distribution",
    x = "Temperature",
    y = "Humidity",
    fill = "Prediction"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Display the plot with density
print(plot_temp_hum_density)
```


#HOUSE DATA - LOADING AND PREPARATION
```{r}
# Load the house dataset
house_data <- read.csv("kc_house_data.csv")

# Display the first rows of the dataset
head(house_data)

# Select relevant variables for the model
house_model_data <- house_data %>%
  select(
    price,
    bedrooms,
    bathrooms,
    sqft_living,
    sqft_lot,
    floors,
    yr_built
  )

summary(house_model_data)

house_sample <- house_model_data %>%
  sample_n(3000)

house_rf <- randomForest(
  price ~ .,
  data = house_sample,
  ntree = 500,
  importance = TRUE
)

print(house_rf)


importance(house_rf)
varImpPlot(house_rf)
```

#PDP — bedrooms

```{r}
pdp_bedrooms <- partial(
  object = house_rf,
  pred.var = "bedrooms",
  train = house_sample,
  grid.resolution = 20
)

plot_bedrooms <- autoplot(pdp_bedrooms) +
  labs(
    title = "PDP - Influence of bedrooms",
    x = "Number of bedrooms",
    y = "Predicted house price"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(plot_bedrooms)

```

#PDP - BATHROOMS
```{r}
pdp_bathrooms <- partial(
  object = house_rf,
  pred.var = "bathrooms",
  train = house_sample,
  grid.resolution = 20
)

plot_bathrooms <- autoplot(pdp_bathrooms) +
  labs(
    title = "PDP - Influence of bathrooms",
    x = "Number of bathrooms",
    y = "Predicted house price"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(plot_bathrooms)
```

#PDP - SQFT LIVING
```{r}
pdp_sqft_living <- partial(
  object = house_rf,
  pred.var = "sqft_living",
  train = house_sample,
  grid.resolution = 50
)

plot_sqft_living <- autoplot(pdp_sqft_living) +
  labs(
    title = "PDP - Influence of living area",
    x = "sqft_living",
    y = "Predicted house price"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(plot_sqft_living)
```


#PDP - FLOORS

```{r}
pdp_floors <- partial(
  object = house_rf,
  pred.var = "floors",
  train = house_sample,
  grid.resolution = 20
)

plot_floors <- autoplot(pdp_floors) +
  labs(
    title = "PDP - Influence of number of floors",
    x = "Number of floors",
    y = "Predicted house price"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(plot_floors)
```
